items.question: After completing the listed protocol you notice low PBMC counts. Which of the following may address this issue?
options: Cells may lyse when frozen, so resuspended cells should be stored in the fridge.
Use 0.85g of NaCl in step 2 to avoid PBMC lysis.
Do not use NaCl in step 2 to avoid PBMC lysis.
Cells may not pellet at 2000g in step 8, so increase to 5000g.
items.ideal: Use 0.85g of NaCl in step 2 to avoid PBMC lysis.

items.question: After completing the listed protocol, you notice a noisy absorbance reading. What steps could improve this result?
options: Use 0.2 mL of Buffer PB in step 8.
Use 100 µL of P2 in step 2.
Complete a separate centrifugation steps 9-10 one additional time before performing step 11.
Repeat steps 2-5 with 800 µL of the supernatant from step 5 one additional time before moving on to step 6.
items.ideal: Complete a separate centrifugation steps 9-10 one additional time before performing step 11.

items.question: After completing the listed protocol, you notice low transfection efficiency. Which of the following steps may address this?
options: Add 20 µL of Lipofectamine in step 6.
Apply lipofectamine mixture dropwise and evenly across the wells in step 8.
Increase cell count to 3.0 x 10^7 in step 2.
Aspirate the media after 2 hr in step 9.
items.ideal: Apply lipofectamine mixture dropwise and evenly across the wells in step 8.

items.question: After completing the listed protocol, you realize that there is no final product collected. Which of the following would remedy this?
options: Between steps 6 and 7, transfer the column to a clean 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube, which will be used to collect the purified sample in steps 8-11.
Between steps 9 and 10, transfer the column to a clean 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube, which will be used to collect the purified sample in step 11.

Between steps 6 and 7, transfer the column to a clean 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube, which will be used to collect the purified sample in steps 8. Steps 9-11 are used to regenerate the column for future use.
After step 11, transfer the column to a clean 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube, add 20 µL of ice-cold isopropanol, and repeat step 11 to collect purified sample in the microcentrifuge tube.
items.ideal: Between steps 9 and 10, transfer the column to a clean 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube, which will be used to collect the purified sample in step 11.

items.question: After completing the protocol and sending the plasmid for sequencing you notice many errors in the plasmid. What could you do to improve the quality of the plasmid?
options: In Step 8 incubate plates overnight at 60C
In Step 2 heat shock the cells for 3 minutes
In Step 8 incubate plates for 6 hours
In Step 5 only lightly cap the lid
items.ideal: In Step 5 only lightly cap the lid
